name: file_read
description:
Read the content of any file with large-file safety, binary detection, UTF-8 validation, and line-offset pagination. Accepts absolute paths, relative paths, paths with ~ or $HOME/$VAR/%VAR%, Windows paths (C:\...), and UNC paths. If the exact path is not found, returns the closest matching file(s) so the agent can self-correct. Returns at most max_lines lines (default 300) starting from offset_line. Use next_offset_line from a truncated response to continue reading.

Validation pipeline: (1) Binary detection (null-bytes) → reject, (2) UTF-8 validation → if invalid, decode with replacement chars + warning flag, (3) Return content.

If max_bytes truncates mid-line, content is trimmed to the last complete line and truncated_mid_line=true. Optional symlink resolution for security-sensitive operations.

path.description: Path to the file to read. Any format is accepted: absolute (/home/user/file.cpp), relative (src/main.cpp), home-relative (~/project/file.cpp), env-var ($HOME/file.cpp, %USERPROFILE%\file.cpp), or Windows (C:\project\file.cpp). Surrounding quotes are stripped automatically.

offset_line.description: 1-based line number to start reading from. Use this to paginate through large files — set to next_offset_line from a previous truncated response.

max_lines.description: Maximum number of lines to return. 0 means no line limit (still bounded by max_bytes). Default 300 lines per read.

max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes to read from the file. Prevents crashes on huge files (logs, binaries). Default 512 KiB (524288). Set to 0 for unlimited.

resolve_symlinks.description: If true, resolves symlinks to their canonical paths (strict mode). If false (default), uses the path as-is. Useful for security-sensitive operations.
